{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",
        "Update harness hooks to enforce QA step",
        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with WebGL testing protocol. Updated session-start.sh and end-of-turn-check.sh hooks. Protocol includes: typecheck, visual test, console check, interaction test, performance check sequence."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking",
        "Study UI patterns: Canva, Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Android",
        "Document all mouse events (click, dblclick, drag, hover, wheel, context)",
        "Document all touch events (tap, pinch, swipe, long-press)",
        "Research cross-platform differences (macOS/Windows/Linux scroll)",
        "Research hardware differences (trackpad vs mouse)",
        "Create docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with all findings",
        "Update harness to preserve context across sessions"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with comprehensive research on: Figma/Google Maps/iOS patterns, mouse/touch/pointer events, wheel normalization (Facebook pattern), momentum physics, cross-platform scroll differences, accessibility requirements. Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "steps": [
        "Track drag velocity during mouse movement",
        "On mouse release, continue panning with velocity",
        "Apply friction to gradually stop",
        "Test on macOS, Windows (if available)",
        "Match Google Maps/iOS feel"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with velocity tracking during drag, friction-based animation loop (0.93 friction), and momentum stops at velocity < 0.5. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "steps": [
        "Add easing to zoom transitions",
        "Implement zoom momentum",
        "Add pinch-to-zoom support for touch devices",
        "Smooth out zoom steps",
        "Test across different input devices"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented quadOut easing for zoom animations (250ms), wheel event accumulation for trackpad smoothing, pinch-to-zoom support via Pointer Events API, animated zoom for +/- buttons and Reset. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "steps": [
        "Add dblclick event handler to canvas",
        "Calculate click position in world coordinates",
        "Zoom 2x centered on click position",
        "Animate zoom transition smoothly",
        "Respect MAX_ZOOM limit"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "BUG: Double-click zoom not implemented in production app (was working in POC F004)."
    },
    {
      "id": "F121",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization",
      "steps": [
        "Research deltaY values across browsers/OS",
        "Normalize scroll sensitivity for macOS/Windows/Linux",
        "Handle trackpad vs mouse differences",
        "Implement adaptive sensitivity based on input type",
        "Test on multiple platforms"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "REQUIRED: Normalize scroll sensitivity across all OS and hardware types."
    },
    {
      "id": "F116",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Performance optimization (60fps)",
      "steps": [
        "Profile with Chrome DevTools",
        "Maintain 60fps during pan/zoom",
        "No memory leaks on extended use",
        "InstancedMesh optimizations",
        "Frustum culling for off-screen tasks"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "Critical for production quality. Can be worked on in parallel with UX features."
    },
    {
      "id": "F117",
      "category": "canvas",
      "priority": 4,
      "description": "Full visual parity with POC reference",
      "steps": [
        "Compare screenshots with design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
        "All 3 LOD layers match visually",
        "Pan/zoom behavior identical",
        "Sidebar and timeline sync correctly",
        "Conflict indicators match"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F116",
      "notes": "Final validation - POC as visual reference"
    },
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Canvas renders and initializes without errors",
      "steps": [
        "Open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html in browser",
        "Canvas displays with header, sidebar, and main area",
        "No JavaScript errors in console",
        "Mock data generates 50 trains with 10 years of tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "POC validated semantic zoom concept. Production app: apps/canvas"
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Pan navigation with drag",
      "steps": [
        "Click and drag on canvas to pan",
        "Cursor changes to grabbing while dragging",
        "Pan works in all directions (X and Y)",
        "Sidebar and timeline header sync with pan position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Implemented with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup events"
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom navigation with scroll wheel",
      "steps": [
        "Scroll up to zoom in, scroll down to zoom out",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor position",
        "Zoom respects MIN_ZOOM (0.1x) and MAX_ZOOM (5x) limits",
        "Zoom level indicator updates in header"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Zoom toward mouse position for natural feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F004",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Double-click zoom",
      "steps": [
        "Double-click zooms in 2x at click location",
        "Works at any current zoom level (respects max)",
        "Animation is smooth (no jarring jump)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Quick zoom in gesture"
    },
    {
      "id": "F005",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom button controls",
      "steps": [
        "Plus button zooms in by 1.5x",
        "Minus button zooms out by 1.5x",
        "Reset button returns to zoom 1.0 and pan origin",
        "Buttons zoom toward center of viewport"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Alternative for users without scroll wheel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F006",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Macro layer renders at zoom < 0.5x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom out below 0.5x to trigger Macro view",
        "Heatmap displays with Yellow-Orange-Red gradient",
        "Cell density reflects task count per month/train",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MACRO' with yellow badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Strategic 10-20 year overview"
    },
    {
      "id": "F007",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Meso layer renders at zoom 0.5x-2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom to 0.5x-2x range to trigger Meso view",
        "Task blocks render as colored rectangles (DNA strips)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MESO' with blue badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Tactical 2-5 year view with task blocks"
    },
    {
      "id": "F008",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Micro layer renders at zoom > 2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom above 2x to trigger Micro view",
        "Task cards render with shadows and rounded corners",
        "Grid lines show days (vertical) and trains (horizontal)",
        "Task type labels visible when card is wide enough",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MICRO' with green badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Operational 4-8 week view with detailed cards"
    },
    {
      "id": "F009",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "LOD transitions are smooth",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom from Macro to Meso - no flickering",
        "Zoom from Meso to Micro - no flickering",
        "State persists correctly across transitions",
        "Performance stays smooth during transition"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F006",
      "notes": "Critical for Google Earth-like UX feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F010",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Sidebar displays train IDs synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Train IDs visible in left sidebar",
        "IDs update as canvas pans vertically",
        "Row height matches canvas zoom level",
        "Only visible trains are rendered (virtual scroll)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Sidebar syncs with panY position"
    },
    {
      "id": "F011",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Timeline header displays dates synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Timeline labels update as canvas pans horizontally",
        "Granularity changes based on zoom (years/months/weeks/days)",
        "Labels are readable and don't overlap",
        "Timeline syncs with panX position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Adaptive granularity: years at low zoom, days at high zoom"
    },
    {
      "id": "F012",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Conflict indicators visible on tasks",
      "steps": [
        "Tasks with conflicts show red border/glow",
        "Conflicts visible in Meso layer (red stroke)",
        "Conflicts visible in Micro layer (red glow shadow)",
        "~5% of tasks have conflict flag for testing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Red highlight draws attention to scheduling issues"
    },
    {
      "id": "F013",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Task colors match design specifications",
      "steps": [
        "Maintenance tasks: Blue gradient (#3b82f6 to #2563eb)",
        "Overhaul tasks: Orange gradient (#f59e0b to #d97706)",
        "Inspection tasks: Gray gradient (#9ca3af to #6b7280)",
        "Gradients render correctly at all zoom levels"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Color coding matches docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md"
    },
    {
      "id": "F014",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "Onboarding instructions display and auto-hide",
      "steps": [
        "Instructions tooltip visible on page load",
        "Shows: 'Scroll to zoom - Drag to pan - Double-click to zoom in'",
        "Auto-fades after 5 seconds",
        "Non-intrusive (pointer-events: none)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Progressive disclosure - teach on first use"
    },
    {
      "id": "F015",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "LOD legend explains zoom levels",
      "steps": [
        "Legend panel visible in bottom-left corner",
        "Shows Macro (yellow), Meso (blue), Micro (green) colors",
        "Describes zoom thresholds (<0.5x, 0.5-2x, >2x)",
        "Helps users understand semantic zoom concept"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Educational UI element"
    },
    {
      "id": "F101",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Create Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS app skeleton",
      "steps": [
        "Create apps/canvas directory with Vite config",
        "Install React 18.3.1, BlueprintJS 6.1.0",
        "Create basic App.tsx with BlueprintJS Navbar",
        "App runs on localhost:5173 without errors",
        "BlueprintJS styles load correctly"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Foundation for production app - React 18 required for BlueprintJS 6"
    },
    {
      "id": "F102",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Add R3F Canvas with OrthographicCamera",
      "steps": [
        "Install @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei",
        "Create InfiniteCanvas component with R3F Canvas",
        "Setup OrthographicCamera for 2D-like view",
        "Canvas fills available space below navbar",
        "No WebGL errors in console"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "R3F 8.x compatible with React 18. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F103",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Basic BlueprintJS layout (Navbar, sidebar placeholder)",
      "steps": [
        "Navbar with app title and zoom indicator",
        "Left sidebar container for train IDs",
        "Main area for R3F canvas",
        "Layout uses flexbox, fills viewport",
        "Responsive to window resize"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "BlueprintJS Card, Navbar, ButtonGroup. Sidebar syncs with canvas panY. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F104",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Data types and mock generator",
      "steps": [
        "Create Train, Task, Config TypeScript interfaces",
        "Implement generateData() function",
        "Generate 50 trains with 10 years of tasks",
        "~5% tasks have conflict flag",
        "Data available via React context or store"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "types.ts + generator.ts in apps/canvas/src/data/. Persistent types use ISO strings, render types use numeric offsets."
    },
    {
      "id": "F105",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MesoLayer - InstancedMesh task blocks",
      "steps": [
        "Create MesoLayer component using InstancedMesh",
        "Render task blocks as colored rectangles",
        "Colors: blue (maintenance), orange (overhaul), gray (inspection)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "Tasks visible at default zoom (1.0x)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F104",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Fixed architectural issue: camera init now uses R3F size (not container div). Tasks aligned with sidebar. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F106",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Pan and Zoom camera controls",
      "steps": [
        "Drag to pan (mousedown + mousemove)",
        "Scroll to zoom (wheel event)",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor",
        "Respect MIN_ZOOM (0.1) and MAX_ZOOM (5.0)",
        "Cursor changes to grab/grabbing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Pan bounded to world limits. Zoom via wheel and buttons. Camera initialized using R3F size (not container div). Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F107",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "LOD state management",
      "steps": [

        "LOD changes trigger layer visibility",
        "State persists across renders",
        "Export state for UI components"

      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Implemented in App.tsx (getLOD, zoom/panY state). Hook extraction optional. Layer visibility tested when F105+ exist."
    },
    {
      "id": "F108",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MacroLayer - Heatmap cells",

        "Only visible when zoom < 0.5x",
        "Smooth transition from MesoLayer"

      "blockedBy": "F105",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Task density aggregated by month. Color gradient: light yellow -> amber -> orange -> red. Year markers included. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },

1→# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log
     2→
     3→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅
     4→
     5→### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE
     6→
     7→**Objective:**
     8→Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.
     9→
    10→**Implementation:**
    11→
    12→1. **Velocity Tracking:**
    13→   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
    14→   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
    15→   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity
    16→
    17→2. **Momentum Animation:**
    18→   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
    19→   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
    20→   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
    21→   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units
    22→
    23→3. **Integration:**
    24→   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
    25→   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
    26→   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation
    27→
    28→**Constants Used:**
    29→```typescript
    30→MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
    31→MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
    32→MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
    33→```
    34→
    35→**Files Changed:**
    36→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
    37→- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)
    38→

    40→- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
    41→- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
    42→- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag

    53→
    54→## Session: 2025-12-16 (UX-AUDIT-001 - UX Research) - COMPLETE ✅
    55→
    56→### UX-AUDIT-001: UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation
    57→
    58→**Objective:**
    59→Research and document UX patterns from industry leaders to inform F118-F121 implementation (momentum panning, smooth zoom, double-click zoom, cross-platform scroll normalization).
    60→
    61→**Research Conducted:**
    62→- Figma: Scroll wheel behavior, Ctrl+scroll zoom, middle-click pan, trackpad gestures
    63→- Google Maps: Momentum physics, gesture handling modes, camera easing (Quadratic.Out)
    64→- Apple Maps/iOS: Momentum for both pan AND zoom, double-tap gestures
    65→- Cross-platform scroll normalization: deltaMode, deltaY variations, Facebook's normalizeWheel pattern
    66→- Trackpad vs mouse detection: Event frequency heuristics
    67→- Pointer Events API: Pinch-to-zoom implementation pattern
    68→
    69→**Deliverables:**
    70→
    71→1. **Created `docs/UX_RESEARCH.md`:**
    72→   - Industry leader patterns (Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Canva)
    73→   - Complete mouse event reference (button values, drag patterns)
    74→   - Touch/Pointer events reference (pinch-to-zoom algorithm)
    75→   - Wheel event normalization (Facebook's algorithm)
    76→   - Momentum/inertia physics implementation
    77→   - Cross-platform considerations (macOS vs Windows scroll)
    78→   - Accessibility requirements (keyboard shortcuts, screen reader)
    79→   - Implementation recommendations for F118-F121
    80→
    81→2. **Updated Documentation:**
    82→   - `docs/README.md`: Added UX_RESEARCH.md to document index
    83→   - `features.json`: UX-AUDIT-001 passes: true
    84→
    85→**Key Findings:**
    86→
    87→| Pattern | Recommendation for MMS |
    88→|---------|------------------------|
    89→| Momentum | Friction 0.93-0.95, stop at velocity < 0.1 |
    90→| Zoom easing | quadOut over 250-350ms |
    91→| Double-click | 2x zoom, 350ms animation, cubicOut |
    92→| Scroll normalization | Use deltaY directly, clamp to ±0.5 per event |
    93→| Trackpad detection | Event frequency < 20ms = trackpad |
    94→
    95→**Sources:**
    96→- [MDN: Pinch zoom gestures](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Pointer_events/Pinch_zoom_gestures)
    97→- [Google Maps: Camera Easing](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/move-camera-ease)
    98→- [Figma Help: Pan and zoom](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500004414582-Pan-and-zoom-in-FigJam)
    99→- [Facebook normalizeWheel.js](https://github.com/facebookarchive/fixed-data-table/blob/master/src/vendor_upstream/dom/normalizeWheel.js)
   100→

   360→4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x
   361→
   362→**Optimizations Implemented:**
   363→
   364→#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
   365→- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
   366→- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
   367→- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)
   368→
   369→#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
   370→- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
   371→- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
   372→- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport
   373→
   374→#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
   375→- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
   376→- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
   377→- Only renders visible rows based on viewport
   378→
   379→#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
   380→- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
   381→- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
   382→- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport
   383→
   384→#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
   385→- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
   386→- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
   387→- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
   388→- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space

   569→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)
   570→
   571→### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar
   572→
   573→**Symptoms:**
   574→- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
   575→- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
   576→- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)
   577→
   578→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   579→- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
   580→- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
   581→- Container div measured: 800x600
   582→- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
   583→- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
   584→- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070
   585→
   586→**Solution:**
   587→- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
   588→- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
   589→- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
   590→- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations
   591→
   592→**Files Changed:**
   593→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
   594→  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
   595→  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
   596→  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
   597→  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
   598→- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
   599→  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
   600→  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds
   601→
   602→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   603→- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
   604→- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
   605→- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
   606→- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
   607→- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
   608→- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
   609→- ✅ No console errors
   610→
   611→**Key Insight:**
   612→When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).
   613→
   614→**Features Updated:**
   615→- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
   616→- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
   617→- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)
   618→
   619→**Current State:**
   620→- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
   621→- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)
   622→- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)
   623→- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (polish)
   624→

   876→### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness
   877→
   878→**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.
   879→
   880→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   881→1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
   882→2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
   883→3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
   884→4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand
   885→
   886→**Solution Implemented:**
   887→
   888→1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   889→   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   890→   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   891→   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
   892→     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
   893→     - Analyze why local docs failed
   894→     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
   895→     - Document in progress.txt
   896→
   897→2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   898→   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   899→   - Added official Anthropic link
   900→   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   901→   - Removed duplicate section from line 293
   902→
   903→**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.
   904→

   907→- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

**Known Issue (pre-existing):**
- Reset View button doesn't fully reset camera position (separate bug, not F118)
- React warning about setState during render (pre-existing, unrelated to momentum)

**Next:** F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (UX-AUDIT-001 - UX Research) - COMPLETE ✅

### UX-AUDIT-001: UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation

**Objective:**
Research and document UX patterns from industry leaders to inform F118-F121 implementation (momentum panning, smooth zoom, double-click zoom, cross-platform scroll normalization).

**Research Conducted:**
- Figma: Scroll wheel behavior, Ctrl+scroll zoom, middle-click pan, trackpad gestures
- Google Maps: Momentum physics, gesture handling modes, camera easing (Quadratic.Out)
- Apple Maps/iOS: Momentum for both pan AND zoom, double-tap gestures
- Cross-platform scroll normalization: deltaMode, deltaY variations, Facebook's normalizeWheel pattern
- Trackpad vs mouse detection: Event frequency heuristics
- Pointer Events API: Pinch-to-zoom implementation pattern

**Deliverables:**

1. **Created `docs/UX_RESEARCH.md`:**
   - Industry leader patterns (Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Canva)
   - Complete mouse event reference (button values, drag patterns)
   - Touch/Pointer events reference (pinch-to-zoom algorithm)
   - Wheel event normalization (Facebook's algorithm)
   - Momentum/inertia physics implementation
   - Cross-platform considerations (macOS vs Windows scroll)
   - Accessibility requirements (keyboard shortcuts, screen reader)
   - Implementation recommendations for F118-F121

2. **Updated Documentation:**
   - `docs/README.md`: Added UX_RESEARCH.md to document index
   - `features.json`: UX-AUDIT-001 passes: true

**Key Findings:**

| Pattern | Recommendation for MMS |
|---------|------------------------|
| Momentum | Friction 0.93-0.95, stop at velocity < 0.1 |
| Zoom easing | quadOut over 250-350ms |
| Double-click | 2x zoom, 350ms animation, cubicOut |
| Scroll normalization | Use deltaY directly, clamp to ±0.5 per event |
| Trackpad detection | Event frequency < 20ms = trackpad |

**Sources:**
- [MDN: Pinch zoom gestures](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Pointer_events/Pinch_zoom_gestures)
- [Google Maps: Camera Easing](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/move-camera-ease)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### Performance Issues Resolved

**Problems Fixed:**
1. ✅ Zoom lagging/slow at high zoom - fixed with frustum culling
2. ✅ Labels only appearing after seconds - fixed with lazy rendering (max 50 labels)
3. ✅ White space around graph at minimum zoom - fixed with dynamic MIN_ZOOM
4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x

**Optimizations Implemented:**

#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)

#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport

#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
- Only renders visible rows based on viewport

#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport

#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (ViewportBounds, effectiveMinZoom, onMinZoomChange)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (complete rewrite with optimizations)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (minZoom state, handleMinZoomChange)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MESO view at 1.0x - task blocks render correctly
- ✅ MICRO view at 2.25x - ~50 labels visible (not 2000), grid lines visible
- ✅ MACRO view at 0.44x - heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ Dynamic MIN_ZOOM at 0.57x - content fills viewport, no excessive white space
- ✅ Sidebar readable at minimum zoom (not scrambled)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Impact:**
| Metric | Before | After | Improvement |
|--------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Grid Lines Draw Calls | 251 | 2 | 99.2% reduction |
| Row Background Draw Calls | 50 | 1 | 98% reduction |
| HTML Labels (MICRO) | ~2000 | max 50 | 97.5% reduction |
| Tasks Rendered | ~5500 all | ~50-200 visible | 96-99% reduction |
| MIN_ZOOM | 0.10x (white space) | 0.57x (fills viewport) | UX improvement |

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)

### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar

**Symptoms:**
- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)

**Root Cause Analysis:**
- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
- Container div measured: 800x600
- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070

**Solution:**
- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
- ✅ No console errors

**Key Insight:**
When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).

**Features Updated:**
- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)

**Current State:**
- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)
- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)
- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (polish)

**Next Session:** F108 (MacroLayer) or F109 (MicroLayer)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (F104 - Data Types & Generator)

**Implemented:**
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces
- `apps/canvas/src/data/generator.ts` - Mock data generation

**Architecture: Persistence vs Rendering**
- Persistent types use ISO 8601 strings for serialization
- Render types use numeric offsets for canvas positioning
- Separation: Domain → Runtime → Rendering

- `Train`, `Task`, `DatasetMeta`, `GeneratedData` (persistent)
- `TaskRender`, `TaskColor`, `TYPE_TO_COLOR` (rendering)
- `ViewConfig`, `ViewportState`, `LODLevel` (canvas config)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)

### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness

**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
     - Analyze why local docs failed
     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
     - Document in progress.txt

2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   - Added official Anthropic link
   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   - Removed duplicate section from line 293

**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.

**Sources:**
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)

### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety

content.text:
The zoom stayed at 5.00x (MAX_ZOOM is respected). All F120 requirements are verified:

**F120 Test Results:**
- ✅ Double-click handler added to canvas
- ✅ Click position calculated in world coordinates
- ✅ Zoom 2x centered on click position (1.00x → 2.00x → 4.00x → 5.00x)
- ✅ Zoom transition animates smoothly (350ms, cubicOut easing)
- ✅ Respects MAX_ZOOM limit (5.00x) - double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x
- ✅ No console errors

The feature was already implemented in F119 (Smooth zoom UX). Let me update the features.json to mark F120 as passing and update the todo list.

content.text: Now let me work on **F121: Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization**. First, let me check what's already implemented and read the UX research document for guidance.

I see the UX research has detailed guidance for F121. The key points for cross-platform scroll normalization are:

1. Normalize `deltaMode` (LINE mode → pixels)
2. Detect trackpad vs mouse by event frequency
3. Apply different sensitivity multipliers
4. Clamp zoom delta per event

Let me check the current implementation in `InfiniteCanvas.tsx` to see what's already there and what needs to be added.

Looking at the current code (lines 744-775), I can see:
- Wheel event accumulation is already implemented (for trackpad smoothing)
- But there's no deltaMode normalization
- No trackpad vs mouse detection
- No sensitivity adjustment

Let me implement the cross-platform scroll normalization:

Now let me add the cross-platform scroll normalization. I need to:

1. Add constants for scroll normalization
2. Add trackpad detection state
3. Create a `normalizeWheelDelta` function
4. Update `handleWheel` to use normalized values

content.text: Now I need to update the `executeAccumulatedZoom` function since it was using the raw delta directly. Let me check it:

I see the issue - `executeAccumulatedZoom` uses `Math.pow(0.995, accumulator.delta)` which works with the old raw delta values. Now that we're storing already-normalized values, I need to adjust this function. The normalized delta is already a zoom change amount, so I should use it differently:

lines: * Execute accumulated zoom with smooth animation
* Called after wheel events are accumulated (for trackpad smoothing)
-    // Calculate zoom factor from accumulated delta
-    // Smooth factor: smaller deltas = smoother zoom
+    // Calculate zoom factor from accumulated normalized delta (F121)
+    // Normalized delta is already scaled by sensitivity, use exponential zoom
+    // Negative delta = zoom in, positive delta = zoom out
const newZoom = Math.max(effectiveMinZoom, Math.min(MAX_ZOOM, currentZoom * zoomFactor));

file.content:
# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

**Known Issue (pre-existing):**
- Reset View button doesn't fully reset camera position (separate bug, not F118)
- React warning about setState during render (pre-existing, unrelated to momentum)

**Next:** F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (UX-AUDIT-001 - UX Research) - COMPLETE ✅

### UX-AUDIT-001: UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation

**Objective:**
Research and document UX patterns from industry leaders to inform F118-F121 implementation (momentum panning, smooth zoom, double-click zoom, cross-platform scroll normalization).

**Objective:**
Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.

**Implementation:**
- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
- ✅ No console errors

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Scroll down (deltaY=300) zooms out: 1.00x → 0.61x
- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
- ✅ No console errors
- ✅ Smooth zoom animation

**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)

---

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

**Known Issue (pre-existing):**
- Reset View button doesn't fully reset camera position (separate bug, not F118)
- React warning about setState during render (pre-existing, unrelated to momentum)

**Next:** F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (UX-AUDIT-001 - UX Research) - COMPLETE ✅

### UX-AUDIT-001: UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation

**Objective:**
Research and document UX patterns from industry leaders to inform F118-F121 implementation (momentum panning, smooth zoom, double-click zoom, cross-platform scroll normalization).

**Research Conducted:**
- Figma: Scroll wheel behavior, Ctrl+scroll zoom, middle-click pan, trackpad gestures
- Google Maps: Momentum physics, gesture handling modes, camera easing (Quadratic.Out)
- Apple Maps/iOS: Momentum for both pan AND zoom, double-tap gestures
- Cross-platform scroll normalization: deltaMode, deltaY variations, Facebook's normalizeWheel pattern
- Trackpad vs mouse detection: Event frequency heuristics
- Pointer Events API: Pinch-to-zoom implementation pattern

**Deliverables:**

1. **Created `docs/UX_RESEARCH.md`:**
   - Industry leader patterns (Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Canva)
   - Complete mouse event reference (button values, drag patterns)
   - Touch/Pointer events reference (pinch-to-zoom algorithm)
   - Wheel event normalization (Facebook's algorithm)
   - Momentum/inertia physics implementation
   - Cross-platform considerations (macOS vs Windows scroll)
   - Accessibility requirements (keyboard shortcuts, screen reader)
   - Implementation recommendations for F118-F121

2. **Updated Documentation:**
   - `docs/README.md`: Added UX_RESEARCH.md to document index
   - `features.json`: UX-AUDIT-001 passes: true

**Key Findings:**

| Pattern | Recommendation for MMS |
|---------|------------------------|
| Momentum | Friction 0.93-0.95, stop at velocity < 0.1 |
| Zoom easing | quadOut over 250-350ms |
| Double-click | 2x zoom, 350ms animation, cubicOut |
| Scroll normalization | Use deltaY directly, clamp to ±0.5 per event |
| Trackpad detection | Event frequency < 20ms = trackpad |

**Sources:**
- [MDN: Pinch zoom gestures](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Pointer_events/Pinch_zoom_gestures)
- [Google Maps: Camera Easing](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/move-camera-ease)
- [Figma Help: Pan and zoom](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500004414582-Pan-and-zoom-in-FigJam)
- [Facebook normalizeWheel.js](https://github.com/facebookarchive/fixed-data-table/blob/master/src/vendor_upstream/dom/normalizeWheel.js)

**Next:** F118 (Momentum panning), F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### Performance Issues Resolved

**Problems Fixed:**
1. ✅ Zoom lagging/slow at high zoom - fixed with frustum culling
2. ✅ Labels only appearing after seconds - fixed with lazy rendering (max 50 labels)
3. ✅ White space around graph at minimum zoom - fixed with dynamic MIN_ZOOM
4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x

**Optimizations Implemented:**

#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)

#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport

#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
- Only renders visible rows based on viewport

#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport

#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (ViewportBounds, effectiveMinZoom, onMinZoomChange)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (complete rewrite with optimizations)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (minZoom state, handleMinZoomChange)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MESO view at 1.0x - task blocks render correctly
- ✅ MICRO view at 2.25x - ~50 labels visible (not 2000), grid lines visible
- ✅ MACRO view at 0.44x - heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ Dynamic MIN_ZOOM at 0.57x - content fills viewport, no excessive white space
- ✅ Sidebar readable at minimum zoom (not scrambled)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F110 - Smooth LOD Transitions)

### F110: Smooth LOD transitions - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**

- `calculateLayerOpacities()` function to compute opacity for each layer based on zoom
- `LODLayers` component to orchestrate layer rendering with opacity

**Technical Details:**
- **LOD Thresholds:** MACRO < 0.5x, MESO 0.5-2x, MICRO > 2x

  - MACRO → MESO: blend zone 0.35-0.65
  - MESO → MICRO: blend zone 1.85-2.15

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)

### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar

**Symptoms:**
- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)

**Root Cause Analysis:**
- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
- Container div measured: 800x600
- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070

**Solution:**
- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
- ✅ No console errors

**Key Insight:**
When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).

**Features Updated:**
- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)

**Current State:**
- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)
- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)
- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (polish)

**Next Session:** F108 (MacroLayer) or F109 (MicroLayer)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)

### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness

**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
     - Analyze why local docs failed
     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
     - Document in progress.txt

2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   - Added official Anthropic link
   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   - Removed duplicate section from line 293

**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.

**Sources:**
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)

### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety

content.text: I see there's a bug report about an "elastic jump" when dragging the canvas. Let me first commit the completed features, then add this bug to the harness.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(canvas): F120 + F121 - double-click zoom + scroll normalization

F120: Double-click to zoom in
- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit
- Already implemented in F119, verified and marked complete

F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization
- deltaMode normalization (LINE/PAGE to pixels)
- Trackpad vs mouse detection by event frequency
- Adaptive sensitivity (mouse: 0.003, trackpad: 0.001)
- Zoom delta clamping (±0.5 per event) to prevent jumps

Constants added:
- LINE_HEIGHT = 40, PAGE_HEIGHT = 800
- MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
- TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001
- MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
- TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20ms

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: Let me look at the drag/pan logic in InfiniteCanvas to identify potential causes of the elastic jump:

content: Deve ser incluido tarefa para adicionar um "mini mapa" do canvas como feito em outros apps que usam essa ideia de Single Pane of Glass para localizar o usuário de aonde ele está no mapa maior (como num jogo).

content.text:
I see the issue. The pan logic calculates the camera position from `cameraPosAtPanStart`, which is captured at the start of the drag. However, there's a potential issue where:

1. When using `cameraPos` from React state in the dependency array, it could have stale values
2. The `clampCameraToWorld` function may cause sudden position changes at boundaries

Let me look more carefully at the issue. The elastic/jump could be caused by:

1. State updates being async (React state batching)
2. Clamping causing sudden jumps at boundaries
3. Velocity tracking using stale values

Let me check if there's an issue with how `cameraPosAtPanStart` is used:
Let me add the minimap feature to the harness and continue investigating the drag bug.

content.text:
Good. Now let me focus on fixing the F122 drag jump bug. Looking at the code, I think the issue might be in the `handlePointerDown` callback. It captures `cameraPos` from React state, but if there's an ongoing momentum animation, the `cameraPos` state might not reflect the actual current position.

Let me trace through the issue more carefully:

I found a key insight. In `executeAccumulatedZoom`, the code correctly checks if there's an ongoing animation and uses the target position. However, in `handlePointerDown`, it directly uses `cameraPos` from state without checking if momentum is active.

The issue is: when the user clicks during momentum animation, `stopMomentum()` stops the animation but `cameraPos` state might not be updated to the final position yet (React batching). Let me fix this:

I see the momentum animation uses `setCameraPos` with a functional update `(prev) => ...`, which is correct. The issue might be that when starting a new pan, we use `cameraPos` from the closure, which could be stale.

Let me look at the `handlePointerDown` more closely and fix it to use a ref for the current camera position:

I see the issue now. The pan calculation uses `cameraPosAtPanStart` which is a React state. When the user starts dragging, `cameraPosAtPanStart` is set to the current `cameraPos`. But if `cameraPos` hasn't been updated by React's batched state updates yet, we could get the wrong start position.

The fix is to use a ref to track the current camera position that's always up to date. Let me add a `cameraPosRef` and keep it synced:

lines: +  // Ref to track camera position synchronously (F122: fix elastic jump)
+  // React state updates are batched/async, but we need immediate access during drag

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me analyze the conversation chronologically:

1. **Session Start**: The harness protocol was triggered, indicating this is a continuation of work on the MMS (Metro Integrated Planning System) canvas project.

2. **Initial State Check**: I read `claude-progress.txt` and `features.json` to understand the current state. The most recent completed features were F118 (Momentum Panning) and F119 (Smooth Zoom).

3. **F120 Investigation**: The next pending feature was F120 (Double-click to zoom in). Upon reading `InfiniteCanvas.tsx`, I discovered that F120 was already implemented (the `handleDoubleClick` function existed at lines 1009-1074).

4. **F120 Testing**: I tested double-click zoom via Playwright MCP:
   - 1.00x → 2.00x (worked)
   - 2.00x → 4.00x (worked)
   - 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM, worked)
   - 5.00x → stayed at 5.00x (MAX_ZOOM respected)

5. **F120 Marked Complete**: Updated features.json to mark F120 as passing.

6. **F121 Implementation**: Started implementing cross-platform scroll normalization. Key changes:
   - Added constants for LINE_HEIGHT, PAGE_HEIGHT, sensitivity values
   - Added trackpad detection state to wheelAccumulatorRef
   - Created `normalizeWheelDelta` function that:
     - Normalizes deltaMode (LINE/PAGE to pixels)
     - Detects trackpad vs mouse by event frequency
     - Applies different sensitivity multipliers
     - Clamps zoom delta per event
   - Updated `handleWheel` to use normalized delta
   - Updated `executeAccumulatedZoom` to use `Math.exp(-accumulator.delta)` instead of `Math.pow(0.995, accumulator.delta)`

7. **F121 Testing**: Tested scroll zoom via Playwright MCP:
   - Scroll down (deltaY=300): 1.00x → 0.61x (zoom out worked)
   - Scroll up (deltaY=-500): 0.61x → 1.00x (zoom in worked)

8. **F121 Marked Complete**: Updated features.json and committed changes.

9. **User Request - Bug Report**: User reported elastic/jump effect when dragging canvas.

10. **F122 Added**: Added new bug fix feature F122 for the elastic jump issue.

11. **User Request - Minimap**: User requested a minimap feature for navigation.

12. **F123 Added**: Added new feature F123 for minimap navigation.

13. **F122 Investigation**: Started investigating the drag jump bug. Found that the issue might be due to React state batching - when `cameraPos` is captured in `handlePointerDown`, it could be stale if momentum animation is running.

14. **F122 Fix Started**: Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously. Was in the process of finding all `setCameraPos` calls to update the ref.

Key files modified:
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Main canvas component with pan/zoom
- `features.json` - Feature tracking
- `claude-progress.txt` - Session progress

User messages (non-tool):
1. "continue" - Initial message to continue work
2. "Inclua no harness tarefa para que seja corrigido bug ao arrastar o canvas/chart - ele está dando algum tipo de "pulo" como se estivesse elatico." - Bug report for elastic jump
3. "Deve ser incluido tarefa para adicionar um "mini mapa" do canvas como feito em outros apps que usam essa ideia de Single Pane of Glass para localizar o usuário de aonde ele está no mapa maior (como num jogo)." - Feature request for minimap

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Initial: Continue work on the MMS canvas project following the harness protocol
   - User reported a bug: Canvas feels elastic/jumpy when dragging ("pulo" como se estivesse elástico)
   - User requested: Add a minimap feature for navigation (like games/design tools)
   - Implement pending features: F120 (double-click zoom), F121 (scroll normalization)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Harness Protocol**: Read progress.txt → features.json → implement → test → commit
   - **Cross-platform scroll normalization**: deltaMode (LINE/PAGE/PIXEL), trackpad vs mouse detection
   - **React state batching**: Issue where async state updates cause stale values in callbacks
   - **Momentum/inertia panning**: Velocity tracking, friction-based animation
   - **R3F (React Three Fiber)**: OrthographicCamera for 2D canvas
   - **Playwright MCP**: Headless browser testing for visual verification

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx`**
     - Main canvas component with pan/zoom/momentum
     - F121: Added scroll normalization constants:
     ```typescript
     // Cross-platform scroll normalization constants (F121)
     const LINE_HEIGHT = 40; // Pixels per line (for deltaMode === 1)
     const PAGE_HEIGHT = 800; // Pixels per page (for deltaMode === 2)
     const MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003; // Base zoom sensitivity for mouse wheel
     const TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001; // Lower sensitivity for trackpad
     const MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5; // Cap zoom change per event
     const TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20; // ms between events
     const TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3; // Rapid events to confirm
     ```
     - F121: Added trackpad detection state:
     ```typescript
     const wheelAccumulatorRef = useRef({
       delta: 0,
       cursorX: 0,
       cursorY: 0,
       timeout: null as ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null,
       // Trackpad detection state
       lastEventTime: 0,
       rapidEventCount: 0,
       isTrackpad: false,
     });
     ```
     - F121: Created `normalizeWheelDelta` function for cross-platform consistency
     - F122: Added cameraPosRef to fix elastic jump:
     ```typescript
     const [cameraPos, setCameraPos] = useState({ x: 0, y: 0 });
     // Ref to track camera position synchronously (F122: fix elastic jump)
     const cameraPosRef = useRef({ x: 0, y: 0 });
     ```

   - **`features.json`**
     - Added F122 (elastic jump bug fix, priority 1)
     - Added F123 (minimap navigation, priority 3)
     - Updated F120 and F121 as passes: true

   - **`claude-progress.txt`**
     - Added session notes for F120 and F121 completion

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **No errors during F120/F121**: Both features tested successfully
   - **F122 investigation in progress**: Identified that React state batching could cause stale `cameraPos` values when starting a new drag during momentum animation

5. Problem Solving:
   - **F120**: Already implemented, just needed verification and marking complete
   - **F121**: Implemented full scroll normalization with deltaMode handling and trackpad detection
   - **F122 (in progress)**: Identified root cause as React state batching; started fix by adding `cameraPosRef`

6. All user messages:
   - "continue" - Initial request to continue harness work
   - "Inclua no harness tarefa para que seja corrigido bug ao arrastar o canvas/chart - ele está dando algum tipo de "pulo" como se estivesse elatico." - Bug report for elastic/jump drag behavior
   - "Deve ser incluido tarefa para adicionar um "mini mapa" do canvas como feito em outros apps que usam essa ideia de Single Pane of Glass para localizar o usuário de aonde ele está no mapa maior (como num jogo)." - Feature request for minimap

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **F122**: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging (IN PROGRESS)
   - **F123**: Minimap navigation (bird's eye view)
   - **F116**: Performance optimization (60fps)
   - **F117**: Visual parity with POC reference

8. Current Work:
   I was fixing F122 (elastic/jump effect when dragging). I had:
   1. Added the bug to features.json
   2. Investigated the code and identified the issue: React state batching causes stale `cameraPos` values
   3. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously
   4. Was about to find all `setCameraPos` calls to also update the ref

   The grep found these locations needing updates:
   - Line 476: `setCameraPos(pos);`
   - Line 591: `setCameraPos(clamped);`
   - Line 665: `setCameraPos((prev) => {...}` (momentum animation)
   - Line 874: `setCameraPos(clamped);`
   - Line 1014: `setCameraPos(clamped);`
   - Line 1134: `setCameraPos(clampedPos);`

9. Optional Next Step:
   Complete F122 fix by updating all `setCameraPos` calls to also sync `cameraPosRef`, then update `handlePointerDown` to use `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` from React state when capturing the pan start position.

   From the grep results, the next immediate step is to update each `setCameraPos` call to also update `cameraPosRef.current`, and then modify `handlePointerDown` (around line 962) to use `cameraPosRef.current`:
   ```typescript
   // Current (problematic):
   setCameraPosAtPanStart(cameraPos);
   // Should become:
   setCameraPosAtPanStart(cameraPosRef.current);
   ```.
Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",
        "Update harness hooks to enforce QA step",
        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with WebGL testing protocol. Updated session-start.sh and end-of-turn-check.sh hooks. Protocol includes: typecheck, visual test, console check, interaction test, performance check sequence."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking",
        "Study UI patterns: Canva, Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Android",
        "Document all mouse events (click, dblclick, drag, hover, wheel, context)",
        "Document all touch events (tap, pinch, swipe, long-press)",
        "Research cross-platform differences (macOS/Windows/Linux scroll)",
        "Research hardware differences (trackpad vs mouse)",
        "Create docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with all findings",
        "Update harness to preserve context across sessions"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with comprehensive research on: Figma/Google Maps/iOS patterns, mouse/touch/pointer events, wheel normalization (Facebook pattern), momentum physics, cross-platform scroll differences, accessibility requirements. Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "steps": [
        "Track drag velocity during mouse movement",
        "On mouse release, continue panning with velocity",
        "Apply friction to gradually stop",
        "Test on macOS, Windows (if available)",
        "Match Google Maps/iOS feel"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with velocity tracking during drag, friction-based animation loop (0.93 friction), and momentum stops at velocity < 0.5. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "steps": [
        "Add easing to zoom transitions",
        "Implement zoom momentum",
        "Add pinch-to-zoom support for touch devices",
        "Smooth out zoom steps",
        "Test across different input devices"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented quadOut easing for zoom animations (250ms), wheel event accumulation for trackpad smoothing, pinch-to-zoom support via Pointer Events API, animated zoom for +/- buttons and Reset. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "steps": [

    {

Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."

lines: -      "notes": "BUG REPORT: Canvas feels elastic or jumpy when dragging. May be related to momentum velocity calculation or camera position clamping."

+      "notes": "COMPLETE - Root cause: React state batching caused stale cameraPos when starting new drag during momentum. Fix: Added cameraPosRef for synchronous position tracking. handlePointerDown now uses ref instead of state. All setCameraPos calls sync to ref via updateCameraPos(). Tested with rapid drag interruptions via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."

{

Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."
    },
    {

      "category": "ux",

    {

    {

Fix: Added cameraPosRef for synchronous position tracking. handlePointerDown now uses ref instead of state. All setCameraPos calls sync to ref via updateCameraPos(). Tested with rapid drag interruptions via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
